title: Separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
friendlyTitle: OpenAI says a major coding benchmark has many flawed tasks
summary: OpenAI audited SWE-Bench Pro and estimated that roughly 30% of its tasks are broken because of issues such as overly strict tests, underspecified prompts, low test coverage, and misleading task descriptions. The company argues that flawed benchmarks can distort model capability and safety decisions.
friendlySummary: OpenAI reviewed SWE-Bench Pro and says about 30% of its coding tasks may be broken or unfair.
whyItMatters: Coding benchmarks influence product claims, procurement, and safety reviews. If popular tests contain large amounts of noise, buyers and builders should treat leaderboard jumps cautiously and demand clearer eval quality controls.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because companies use benchmarks to decide which AI coding tools to trust. Bad tests can lead to bad decisions.
